source.public_results_title: Dynasticism and public attitudes in Taiwan
notes: Reference values are manually transcribed from public sources.
Some items are exact from Table 2 in the public paper; others are approximate topline shares described in prose.
Unmatched sample questions are intentionally left without public reference rather than back-filled from inference outputs.

questions.question_text: 下面哪一個最像是我國民主制度最嚴重的問題？
questions.notes: Table 2 in Schafferer (2024), based on TEDS2018_SE01, N=1008.

questions.question_text: 如果您聽到一個新的候選人來自政治家庭，您對他的觀感是好、不好，還是沒差？
questions.notes: Public paper prose: 1.5% positive, 41.3% negative, the remainder indifferent.

questions.question_text: 政治家庭出身的候選人在選舉上更有優勢，這是不公平的。
questions.notes: Public paper prose: almost eight out of ten voters see inherited incumbency advantage as unfair.

questions.question_text: 哪一種候選人比較可能會有貪污行為？
questions.notes: Public paper prose: close to 90% think political-family candidates are more likely to engage in corruption.

questions.question_text: 哪一種候選人比較能做事？
questions.notes: Public paper prose: only about one third believe legacy candidates are more capable of getting things done.

questions.question_text: 哪一種候選人比較能為地方爭取得到較多資源？
questions.notes: Public paper prose: almost 80% think legacy politicians are more likely to secure resources for their constituencies.
